narrative: All three watchlist coins extended their Iran de-escalation recovery Saturday — bitcoin climbed to $63,665 and traders eye $75,000 as Trump ceasefire signals and SpaceX's record-breaking $75 billion IPO lifted global risk assets per CoinDesk. The post-IPO story has a crypto dimension: SpaceX stock launched simultaneously on Solana alongside its Nasdaq debut, while Citi opened tokenized private-market access and Canton Network raised $355 million to bring capital markets onchain per CoinDesk. The uptick masks thin institutional demand — Sygnum's CIO attributes recent bitcoin ETF outflows to arbitrage unwinds rather than IPO rotation, and analysts warn bitcoin has entered a "deep bear-market valuation zone" per CoinDesk.
worth_watching: BlackRock income-paying bitcoin ETF — iShares Bitcoin Premium Income ETF nears launch, generating yield by selling call options on IBIT at a fee that undercuts rivals per CoinDesk; a new income structure on spot bitcoin not yet in watchlist metrics
LG Electronics onchain (Arbitrum) — LG built a blockchain-based advertising platform with Arbitrum per CoinDesk; a major consumer brand integrating blockchain for real commercial infrastructure

Prediction markets regulation — Former SEC/CFTC chair Gensler argued prediction markets can't preempt state laws while the CFTC issued its first proposed rule for such contracts per CoinDesk; outcome shapes whether these platforms can scale in the U.S.
